title: verbascum serpenticola
intro: Verbascum serpenticola, also known as the thread-leaved mullein, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities, which make it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Verbascum serpenticola include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Verbascum serpenticola is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
